# Web Framework, Middleware, and gRPC Parity Map

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.5.1` ([T5.1])
**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
**Maintained by**: WhiteDesert (codex-cli / gpt-5-codex)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Version**: 1.6.0
**Dependencies**: T1.3.c (roadmap baseline)
**Purpose**: Capability-level parity map for high-level application-stack ergonomics and production behaviors versus axum, warp, tower-http, and tonic. Covers migration blockers and ownership boundaries per module.

---

## 1. Scope

This map covers 5 Tokio-ecosystem surfaces against Asupersync web/service/grpc/http module groups:

| Tokio Crate | Asupersync Module | LOC | Status |
|-------------|-------------------|-----|--------|
| axum (router, extractors, responses) | `src/web/` | ~4,882 | Active |
| warp (filters, rejects/recover, ws, multipart) | `src/web/`, `src/service/` | Shared with web stack (~5,757) | Active |
| tower-http (middleware layers) | `src/service/`, `src/web/middleware.rs` | ~5,353 + 875 | Active |
| tonic (gRPC client/server) | `src/grpc/` | ~6,750 | Active |
| hyper (HTTP transport) | `src/http/` | ~22,831 | Active |

**Non-overlapping application-stack LOC**: ~40,691

---

## 2. Web Framework Parity (vs axum)

### 2.1 Router

| axum Feature | Asupersync | Location | Status | Gap ID |
|-------------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| `Router::new()` | `Router::new()` | `web/router.rs` | Complete | — |
| `.route("/path", method_router)` | `.route("/path", get(h).post(h))` | `web/router.rs` | Complete | — |
| `.nest("/prefix", router)` | `.nest("/prefix", routes)` | `web/router.rs` | Complete | — |
| `.merge(router)` | — | — | Missing | WEB-G1 |
| `.layer(middleware)` | `.layer(middleware)` via MiddlewareStack | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `.with_state(state)` | `State<T>` extractor | `web/extract.rs` | Complete | — |
| `.fallback(handler)` | `.fallback(handler)` | `web/router.rs` | Complete | — |
| Method routing (get/post/put/delete/patch/head/options) | All 7 methods | `web/router.rs` | Complete | — |
| Path parameters (`:id`) | `Path<T>` extractor | `web/extract.rs` | Complete | — |
| Wildcard routes (`*path`) | — | — | Missing | WEB-G3 |
| Route ordering / priority | — | — | Missing | WEB-G4 |

|---------------|-----------|--------|--------|

| `Extension<T>` | `Extensions` | Complete | — |
| `RawBody` / `Bytes` | `RawBody` | Complete | — |
| `HeaderMap` / `TypedHeader` | — | Missing | WEB-G5 |
| `ConnectInfo<T>` | — | Missing | WEB-G6 |
| `MatchedPath` | — | Missing | WEB-G7 |
| `OriginalUri` | — | Missing | WEB-G7 |
| `Host` | — | Missing | WEB-G7 |
| `Multipart` | — | Missing | WEB-G8 |
| `WebSocketUpgrade` | Separate module | `net/websocket/` | Partial | WEB-G9 |
| `FromRequestParts` trait | `FromRequestParts` trait | Complete | — |
| Rejection types | — | Missing | WEB-G11 |

### 2.3 Responses

| axum Response | Asupersync | Status | Gap ID |
|--------------|-----------|--------|--------|
| `IntoResponse` trait | `IntoResponse` trait | Complete | — |
| `StatusCode` | `StatusCode` | Complete | — |
| `Json<T>` response | `Json<T>` | Complete | — |
| `Html<T>` | `Html<T>` | Complete | — |
| `Redirect` | `Redirect` | Complete | — |
| Tuple responses `(StatusCode, body)` | Tuple responses | Complete | — |

| `AppendHeaders` | — | Missing | WEB-G14 |

### 2.4 Filter-Based Web Framework Parity (vs warp)

| warp Feature | Asupersync | Location | Status | Gap ID |
|-------------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| `warp::path!` typed path composition | Router path params + manual route composition | `web/router.rs`, `web/extract.rs` | Partial | WEB-G15 |
| Filter combinators (`and`, `or`, `map`, `and_then`) | Router + middleware stack + service layers | `web/router.rs`, `web/middleware.rs`, `service/builder.rs` | Partial | WEB-G15 |
| `warp::any().map(...)` state injection | `State<T>` extractor + layer state | `web/extract.rs` | Complete | — |
| `warp::reply::json`, status replies | `Json<T>`, `IntoResponse` | `web/response.rs` | Complete | — |
| `warp::reject` + `recover` taxonomy | Rejection/error mapping surface not fully aligned | `web/error.rs`, `web/router.rs` | Partial | WEB-G11 |
| `warp::cors()` filter | `CorsMiddleware` + `CorsPolicy` | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `warp::fs::dir` / `warp::fs::file` | No static-serving middleware | — | Missing | MW-G7 |
| `warp::multipart::form` | No multipart extractor | — | Missing | WEB-G8 |
| `warp::ws()` upgrade filter | WebSocket module exists, but no first-class web extractor integration | `net/websocket/`, `web/extract.rs` | Partial | WEB-G9 |
| `BoxedFilter` ergonomic type erasure | No boxed filter equivalent | — | Missing | WEB-G16 |

### 2.5 T5.2 Router Composition and Route-Matching Closure Contract

The following contract rows close the scoped T5.2 parity requirements for
router composition and route-matching behavior.

| Contract ID | Behavior | Success Path | Failure Path | Cancellation Path | Deterministic Assertion |
|-------------|----------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|-------------------------|
| `T52-ROUTE-01` | Nested routing (`nest`) preserves prefix isolation | Nested route resolves with expected handler and params | Prefix mismatch falls through without cross-prefix leakage | Parent region cancel aborts in-flight nested handler cleanly | Replay log confirms identical prefix resolution order |
| `T52-ROUTE-02` | Router composition (`merge`) is deterministic | Merged routers dispatch in stable precedence order | Duplicate route/method collision emits explicit conflict diagnostics | Cancel before dispatch returns cancelled outcome without invoking handler | Deterministic tie-break key is route registration index |
| `T52-ROUTE-03` | Fallback resolution semantics | Unmatched request resolves to configured fallback chain | Missing fallback returns explicit 404 contract response | Cancel during fallback body generation drains response task | Fallback decision path recorded with route fingerprint |
| `T52-ROUTE-04` | Method dispatch correctness | Supported method maps to exact route handler | Unsupported method yields 405 with allow-list metadata | Cancel between method check and handler poll returns cancelled outcome | Method map and allow-list are stable across runs |
| `T52-ROUTE-05` | Wildcard + parameter extraction precedence | Most-specific route wins over wildcard captures | Ambiguous patterns surface deterministic conflict status | Cancel during extraction does not leak partial param state | Extracted param set hash is stable for same path input |
| `T52-ROUTE-06` | Route ordering and priority invariants | Static > param > wildcard precedence remains invariant | Invalid reordering request rejected by router config gate | Cancel while rebuilding route table leaves prior table intact | Route table checksum unchanged on cancelled mutation |

### 2.6 T5.2 Deterministic Scenario Pack

| Scenario ID | Scope | Expected Status | Required Log Fields |
|-------------|-------|-----------------|---------------------|
| `T52-ROUTE-01` | Nested prefix hit | success | `scenario_id`, `route_fingerprint`, `method`, `outcome_class` |
| `T52-ROUTE-02` | Nested prefix miss + fallback | success | `scenario_id`, `fallback_selected`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T52-ROUTE-03` | Merge conflict detection | failure | `scenario_id`, `conflict_route`, `conflict_reason`, `outcome_class` |
| `T52-ROUTE-04` | Wildcard vs param precedence | success | `scenario_id`, `match_order`, `param_map_hash`, `outcome_class` |
| `T52-ROUTE-05` | Unsupported method dispatch | failure | `scenario_id`, `allow_header`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T52-ROUTE-06` | Cancellation before handler enter | cancelled | `scenario_id`, `cancel_requested`, `cancel_drained`, `outcome_class` |
| `T52-ROUTE-07` | Cancellation during fallback generation | cancelled | `scenario_id`, `fallback_selected`, `cancel_drained`, `outcome_class` |
| `T52-ROUTE-08` | Route-table rebuild cancellation safety | cancelled | `scenario_id`, `route_table_checksum_before`, `route_table_checksum_after`, `outcome_class` |

### 2.7 T5.3 Extractor and Body Handling Closure Contract

The following contract rows close the scoped T5.3 parity requirements for
request extraction and body decoding semantics across all built-in extractors.

| Contract ID | Behavior | Success Path | Failure Path | Cancellation Path | Deterministic Assertion |
|-------------|----------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|-------------------------|
| `T53-EXTRACT-01` | `Path<T>` extraction from route params | Single and multi-param extraction return correct values | Missing params return 400 with descriptive message | Cancel before extraction completes returns without leaking partial params | Param map hash stable for identical route + path input |
| `T53-EXTRACT-02` | `Query<T>` percent-decoding and parsing | Key-value pairs decoded including `+`-as-space and `%XX` escapes | Empty query string returns empty map (not error) | Cancel during parsing does not leak intermediate state | Decoded output byte-identical for same input across runs |
| `T53-EXTRACT-03` | `Json<T>` body size enforcement | Valid JSON under 10 MiB limit deserializes correctly | Body > `MAX_JSON_BODY_SIZE` returns 413 Payload Too Large | Cancel before body read completes drops body without deserialization | Size check precedes deserialization (no partial parse on oversize) |
| `T53-EXTRACT-04` | `Json<T>` content-type validation | `application/json` content-type accepted (including charset suffixes) | Wrong content-type returns 415 Unsupported Media Type | N/A (synchronous check) | Content-type match is case-sensitive substring of header value |
| `T53-EXTRACT-05` | `Json<T>` deserialization error handling | Well-formed JSON matching target type succeeds | Malformed JSON or type mismatch returns 422 Unprocessable Entity | N/A (synchronous) | Error message includes serde_json error context |
| `T53-EXTRACT-06` | `Form<T>` body size enforcement | Valid form body under 2 MiB limit parses correctly | Body > `MAX_FORM_BODY_SIZE` returns 413 Payload Too Large | Cancel before body read drops body without parsing | Size check precedes content-type and UTF-8 validation |
| `T53-EXTRACT-07` | `Form<T>` content-type and encoding validation | `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` accepted; UTF-8 body parses | Wrong content-type returns 415; invalid UTF-8 returns 400 | N/A (synchronous) | Error status codes match axum rejection semantics |
| `T53-EXTRACT-08` | `State<T>` typed state extraction | State injected via `Router::with_state()` resolves via TypeId | Missing state returns 500 Internal Server Error with type name | N/A (synchronous) | TypeId lookup is deterministic; same type always matches |
| `T53-EXTRACT-09` | `RawBody` always succeeds | Any body (including empty) extracts as `Bytes` | Never fails (infallible extractor) | Cancel before body read returns empty body | Body bytes identical to request input |
| `T53-EXTRACT-10` | `FromRequestParts` / `FromRequest` trait hierarchy | `FromRequestParts` impls work without consuming body; blanket impl bridges to `FromRequest` | Extraction errors carry status code and message | Cancel propagates through the blanket impl without double-extraction | Multiple `FromRequestParts` extractors compose without ordering dependencies |

| Scenario ID | Scope | Expected Status | Required Log Fields |
|-------------|-------|-----------------|---------------------|
| `T53-EXTRACT-01` | Path single-param extraction | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `param_count`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-02` | Path multi-param extraction | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `param_map_hash`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-03` | Path missing params | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `status_code`, `error_message`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-04` | Query with percent-encoding | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `decoded_count`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-05` | Query empty string | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `decoded_count`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-06` | Json valid deserialization | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `body_size`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-07` | Json body too large | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `body_size`, `limit`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-08` | Json wrong content-type | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `content_type`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-09` | Json malformed body | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `parse_error`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-10` | Form valid submission | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `field_count`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-11` | Form body too large | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `body_size`, `limit`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-12` | Form wrong content-type | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `content_type`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-13` | Form invalid UTF-8 body | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `status_code`, `error_message`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-14` | State present | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `state_type`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-15` | State missing | failure | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `state_type`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-16` | RawBody extraction (empty) | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `body_size`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-17` | RawBody extraction (non-empty) | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `body_size`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-18` | Headers extraction | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `header_count`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-19` | Cancellation during body-consuming extraction | cancelled | `scenario_id`, `extractor`, `cancel_requested`, `body_drained`, `outcome_class` |
| `T53-EXTRACT-20` | Multiple FromRequestParts extractors compose | success | `scenario_id`, `extractor_count`, `all_extracted`, `outcome_class` |

## 3. Middleware Parity (vs tower-http)

### 3.1 Service Trait

| tower Feature | Asupersync | Location | Status | Gap ID |
|--------------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| `Service<Request>` trait | `Service<Request>` + `AsupersyncService` | `service/service.rs` | Complete | — |
| `Layer` trait | `Layer` trait | `service/layer.rs` | Complete | — |
| `ServiceBuilder` | `ServiceBuilder` | `service/builder.rs` | Complete | — |
| `ServiceExt` (ready, oneshot) | `ServiceExt` | `service/service.rs` | Complete | — |
| Tower adapter (bidirectional) | `TowerAdapter` / `AsupersyncAdapter` | `service/service.rs` | Complete | — |

### 3.2 Middleware Layers

| tower-http Layer | Asupersync | Location | Status | Gap ID |
|-----------------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| `TimeoutLayer` | `TimeoutLayer` | `service/timeout.rs` | Complete | — |
| `RateLimitLayer` | `RateLimitLayer` | `service/rate_limit.rs` | Complete | — |
| `ConcurrencyLimitLayer` | `ConcurrencyLimitLayer` | `service/concurrency_limit.rs` | Complete | — |
| `LoadShedLayer` | `LoadShedLayer` | `service/load_shed.rs` | Complete | — |
| `RetryLayer` | `RetryLayer` | `service/retry.rs` | Complete | — |
| `CompressionLayer` | `CompressionMiddleware` | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `DecompressionLayer` | — | — | Missing | MW-G1 |
| `CorsLayer` | `CorsMiddleware` + `CorsPolicy` | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `TraceLayer` | — | — | Missing | MW-G3 |
| `PropagateHeaderLayer` | — | — | Missing | MW-G4 |
| `SetRequestHeaderLayer` | — | — | Missing | MW-G4 |
| `SetResponseHeaderLayer` | `SetResponseHeaderMiddleware` | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `AddAuthorizationLayer` | `AuthMiddleware` (Bearer/Basic/ApiKey) | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `ValidateRequestHeaderLayer` | `AuthMiddleware` (validate mode) | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `NormalizePath` | `NormalizePathMiddleware` | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `ServeDir` / `ServeFile` | — | — | Missing | MW-G7 |
| `RequestBodyLimitLayer` | `RequestBodyLimitMiddleware` | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `CatchPanicLayer` | `CatchPanicMiddleware` | `web/middleware.rs` | Complete | — |
| `MapResponseBodyLayer` | — | — | Missing | MW-G10 |
| `MapRequestBodyLayer` | — | — | Missing | MW-G10 |
| `FollowRedirectLayer` | — | — | Missing | MW-G11 |

### 3.3 Combinator-Based Middleware (Asupersync-Specific)

Asupersync's `web/middleware.rs` provides combinator-pattern middleware that integrates the resilience library:

| Middleware | Purpose | tower-http Equivalent |
|-----------|---------|----------------------|
| `CircuitBreakerMiddleware` | Circuit breaker | None (external crate) |
| `BulkheadMiddleware` | Concurrency isolation | None (manual pattern) |
| `RetryMiddleware` | Retry with backoff | `RetryLayer` (similar) |
| `RateLimitMiddleware` | Token bucket | `RateLimitLayer` (similar) |
| `TimeoutMiddleware` | Deadline enforcement | `TimeoutLayer` (similar) |
| `MiddlewareStack` | Composable builder | `ServiceBuilder` (similar) |

### 3.4 T5.4 Middleware Stack Closure Contract

The following contract rows close the scoped T5.4 parity requirements for
middleware composition and common production-concern behaviors.

| Contract ID | Behavior | Success Path | Failure Path | Cancellation Path | Deterministic Assertion |
|-------------|----------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|-------------------------|
| `T54-MW-01` | CorsMiddleware preflight handling | OPTIONS with valid origin returns 204 with CORS headers (allow-origin, allow-methods, allow-headers, max-age) | Origin not in `CorsAllowOrigin::Exact` set: request passes through without CORS headers | Cancel during inner handler does not affect preflight short-circuit path | CORS header set is fully determined by `CorsPolicy` + request origin |
| `T54-MW-02` | CorsMiddleware simple/actual request | Non-preflight with valid origin gets allow-origin + vary + expose-headers on response | Origin not allowed: inner handler called but no CORS headers applied | Cancel propagated to inner handler; partial response not decorated | Vary header always includes `origin` for origin-sensitive policies |
| `T54-MW-03` | CompressionMiddleware content negotiation | Accept-Encoding matched against server supported set; body compressed; content-encoding header set; vary header updated | No acceptable encoding and Accept-Encoding present: returns 406 Not Acceptable | Cancel before compression completes returns uncompressed response | Encoding selection is deterministic for same Accept-Encoding + config |
| `T54-MW-04` | CompressionMiddleware min-body-size threshold | Body below `min_body_size` bypasses compression entirely | N/A (always succeeds — small bodies pass through) | N/A (synchronous check) | Threshold comparison is `<`, not `<=` (body.len() < min_body_size) |
| `T54-MW-05` | AuthMiddleware bearer token validation | Valid bearer token in Authorization header passes through to inner handler | Missing or invalid token returns 401 with `www-authenticate: Bearer` | N/A (synchronous check before handler invocation) | Token match is exact string comparison for `ExactBearer` |
| `T54-MW-06` | NormalizePathMiddleware trailing-slash strategies | `Trim` removes trailing `/`; `Always` appends `/`; root path `/` preserved | `RedirectTrim`/`RedirectAlways` return 301 with Location header to canonical form | Cancel during redirect does not invoke inner handler | Normalization is idempotent: applying twice yields same path |
| `T54-MW-07` | RequestBodyLimitMiddleware enforcement | Body within limit passes through to inner handler unchanged | Body > `max_bytes` returns 413 Payload Too Large with size details | N/A (synchronous check before handler invocation) | Size check precedes handler invocation (no partial processing) |
| `T54-MW-08` | CatchPanicMiddleware recovery | Non-panicking handler returns normal response | Panicking handler caught by `catch_unwind`; returns 500 Internal Server Error without exposing panic details | Panic during cancel-path caught identically | Panic payload is never included in response body (information leak prevention) |
| `T54-MW-09` | TimeoutMiddleware deadline enforcement | Handler completing within timeout returns normal response | Handler exceeding timeout returns 504 Gateway Timeout with elapsed duration | Cancel propagated through timeout wrapper | Timeout comparison uses `>` on elapsed wall-clock (Phase 0 post-hoc check) |
| `T54-MW-10` | CircuitBreakerMiddleware state transitions | Closed state: handler invoked normally; success keeps circuit closed | Open state: 503 with retry-after header; HalfOpenFull: 503 max probes | Cancel during half-open probe does not count as success or failure | Circuit state is determined by failure count and recovery timeout |
| `T54-MW-11` | RateLimitMiddleware token bucket | Token available: handler invoked; token consumed | No tokens: 429 Too Many Requests with retry-after header | N/A (synchronous acquire before handler) | Token refill is deterministic for given time progression |
| `T54-MW-12` | BulkheadMiddleware concurrency isolation | Permit acquired: handler invoked; permit released on completion | No permits: 503 Service Unavailable | Cancel during handler execution still releases permit via guard | Permit count is atomic; no double-release or leak possible |
| `T54-MW-13` | RetryMiddleware exponential backoff | First attempt success: single handler invocation | All attempts fail: returns last 5xx response; non-idempotent methods skipped by default | Cancel during retry sleep does not invoke further attempts | Retry count and delay progression determined by `RetryPolicy` |
| `T54-MW-14` | MiddlewareStack composition ordering | Layers applied in builder order (outermost first); `.build()` returns composed Handler | Empty stack (no middleware) returns inner handler unchanged | Cancel at any layer propagates inward without skipping cleanup | Composition is associative: stack order fully determines dispatch order |
| `T54-MW-15` | LoadShedMiddleware overload protection | In-flight count below threshold: handler invoked; counter decremented via guard on completion | In-flight >= `max_in_flight`: 503 Service Unavailable; counter not incremented | Cancel during handler still decrements via `InFlightGuard` drop | Counter uses `AcqRel` ordering for cross-thread visibility |
| `T54-MW-16` | RequestIdMiddleware generation and propagation | Existing request-id header propagated; missing header generates monotonic `req-N` ID | N/A (infallible middleware) | Cancel after ID generation still sets response header | Counter is `AtomicU64` with `Relaxed` ordering (uniqueness, not strict order) |
| `T54-MW-17` | SetResponseHeaderMiddleware injection modes | `Always` mode overwrites existing header; `IfMissing` mode preserves existing | N/A (infallible middleware) | Cancel during inner handler may produce partial response; header still injected | Mode selection is static per middleware instance |

### 3.5 T5.4 Deterministic Scenario Pack

| Scenario ID | Scope | Expected Status | Required Log Fields |
|-------------|-------|-----------------|---------------------|
| `T54-MW-01` | CORS preflight with allowed origin | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `origin`, `preflight`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-02` | CORS preflight with disallowed origin | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `origin`, `cors_headers_applied`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-03` | CORS actual request with credentials | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `allow_credentials`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-04` | Compression with matching Accept-Encoding | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `encoding`, `body_size`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-05` | Compression below min-body-size | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `body_size`, `threshold`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-06` | Compression with no acceptable encoding | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `accept_encoding`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-07` | Auth valid bearer token | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `auth_scheme`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-08` | Auth missing token | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `status_code`, `www_authenticate`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-09` | NormalizePath trailing slash trim | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `original_path`, `normalized_path`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-10` | NormalizePath redirect mode | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `redirect_status`, `location`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-11` | RequestBodyLimit within limit | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `body_size`, `limit`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-12` | RequestBodyLimit exceeded | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `body_size`, `limit`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-13` | CatchPanic handler panics | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `panic_caught`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-14` | Timeout within deadline | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `elapsed_ms`, `timeout_ms`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-15` | Timeout exceeded | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `elapsed_ms`, `timeout_ms`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-16` | CircuitBreaker open state rejection | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `circuit_state`, `retry_after`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-17` | RateLimit token exhausted | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `retry_after`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-18` | Bulkhead concurrency exhausted | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-19` | Retry all attempts fail | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `attempt_count`, `last_status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-20` | MiddlewareStack composition order verification | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `layer_count`, `execution_order`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-21` | LoadShed at capacity | failure | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `in_flight`, `max_in_flight`, `status_code`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-22` | RequestId generation (no existing header) | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `generated_id`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-23` | SetResponseHeader always vs if-missing modes | success | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `mode`, `header_name`, `outcome_class` |
| `T54-MW-24` | Cancellation during bulkhead handler releases permit | cancelled | `scenario_id`, `middleware`, `permit_released`, `cancel_drained`, `outcome_class` |

---

## 4. gRPC Parity (vs tonic)

### 4.1 Server

| tonic Feature | Asupersync | Location | Status | Gap ID |
|--------------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| `Server::builder()` | `ServerBuilder` | `grpc/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Service registration | Service dispatch | `grpc/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Unary RPC | `UnaryMethod` trait | `grpc/service.rs` | Complete | — |
| Server streaming | `ServerStreamingMethod` | `grpc/service.rs` | Complete | — |
| Client streaming | `ClientStreamingMethod` | `grpc/service.rs` | Complete | — |
| Bidirectional streaming | `BidiStreamingMethod` | `grpc/service.rs` | Complete | — |
| Health check service | `HealthService` | `grpc/health.rs` | Complete | — |
| Interceptors | `InterceptorLayer` | `grpc/interceptor.rs` | Complete | — |
| Metadata (headers) | `Metadata` | `grpc/streaming.rs` | Complete | — |
| Max message size config | `ServerConfig` | `grpc/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Reflection service | `ReflectionService` | `grpc/reflection.rs` | Complete | ~~GRPC-G1~~ |
| TLS configuration | Via `tls/acceptor.rs` | — | Complete | — |
| Graceful shutdown | Via `server/shutdown.rs` | — | Complete | — |
| Compression (gzip) | `gzip_frame_compress` / `gzip_frame_decompress` | `grpc/codec.rs` (feature `compression`) | Complete | ~~GRPC-G2~~ |
| gRPC-Web (binary + text) | `WebFrameCodec`, `TrailerFrame` | `grpc/web.rs` | Complete | — |
| Server compression config | `send_compression`, `accept_compression` | `grpc/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Protobuf codegen (build.rs) | — | — | Missing | GRPC-G3 |
| `#[tonic::async_trait]` macro | — | — | Missing | GRPC-G4 |

| tonic Feature | Asupersync | Location | Status | Gap ID |
|--------------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| `Channel` (connection) | `Channel` / `ChannelBuilder` | `grpc/client.rs` | Complete | — |
| Unary call | `GrpcClient` methods | `grpc/client.rs` | Complete | — |
| Server streaming | `ResponseStream` | `grpc/client.rs` | Complete | — |
| Client streaming | — | — | Partial | GRPC-G5 |
| Bidirectional streaming | — | — | Partial | GRPC-G5 |
| Load balancing | — | — | Missing | GRPC-G6 |
| Connection pooling | — | — | Missing | GRPC-G7 |
| Interceptors (client-side) | `FnInterceptor` | `grpc/interceptor.rs` | Partial | — |
| Retry policy | — | — | Missing | GRPC-G8 |

| Protocol Feature | Asupersync | Status | Gap ID |
|-----------------|-----------|--------|--------|

| Status codes (16 codes) | `Status` | Complete | — |

| Error details (google.rpc.Status) | — | Missing | GRPC-G10 |

## 5. HTTP Transport Parity (vs hyper)

### 5.1 HTTP/1.1

| Feature | Asupersync | Status |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| Request/response parsing | `http/h1/codec.rs` | Complete |
| Chunked transfer encoding | `http/h1/codec.rs` | Complete |
| Keep-alive | `http/h1/client.rs` | Complete |
| Pipelining | `http/h1/client.rs` | Complete |
| 100-continue | `http/h1/server.rs` (Expect header handling + 417 rejection) | Complete (~~HT-G1~~) |
| Upgrade (WebSocket) | `net/websocket/handshake.rs` | Complete |
| Body streaming | `http/body.rs` | Complete |

### 5.2 HTTP/2

| Feature | Asupersync | Status |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| Stream multiplexing | `http/h2/stream.rs` | Complete |
| HPACK compression | `http/h2/hpack.rs` | Complete |
| Flow control | `http/h2/connection.rs` | Complete |
| SETTINGS negotiation | `http/h2/settings.rs` | Complete |
| PING/GOAWAY | `http/h2/frame.rs` | Complete |
| RST_STREAM rate limiting | `http/h2/connection.rs` | Complete |
| Server push | — | Missing (HT-G2) |
| Priority/dependency | — | Missing (HT-G3) |

### 5.3 Connection Pooling

| Feature | Asupersync | Status |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| Per-host pooling | `http/pool.rs` | Complete |
| Idle timeout eviction | `http/pool.rs` | Complete |
| Health checks | `http/pool.rs` | Complete |
| Per-host and global limits | `http/pool.rs` | Complete |

### 5.4 Server Lifecycle Parity

| hyper/axum Feature | Asupersync | Location | Status | Gap ID |
|-------------------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| `Server::bind()` | `Http1Listener::bind()` | `http/h1/listener.rs` | Complete | — |
| Graceful shutdown | `ShutdownSignal` (4-phase: Running→Draining→ForceClosing→Stopped) | `server/shutdown.rs` | Complete | — |
| Connection drain with timeout | `ConnectionManager::drain_with_stats()` | `server/connection.rs` | Complete | — |
| Connection capacity limit | `max_connections` (default 10,000) | `server/connection.rs` | Complete | — |
| Per-connection request limit | `max_requests_per_connection` (default 1,000) | `http/h1/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Idle timeout | `idle_timeout` (default 1 min) | `http/h1/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Keep-alive control | `keep_alive` flag + `Connection` header | `http/h1/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Header/body size limits | `max_headers_size` (64KB) / `max_body_size` (16MB) | `http/h1/server.rs` | Complete | — |
| Transient accept error backoff | Exponential backoff (2ms→64ms) | `http/h1/listener.rs` | Complete | — |
| RAII connection guard | `ConnectionGuard` auto-deregister | `server/connection.rs` | Complete | — |
| Shutdown statistics | `ShutdownStats` (drained/force-closed/duration) | `server/shutdown.rs` | Complete | — |
| `with_graceful_shutdown` helper | `with_graceful_shutdown()` | `signal/graceful.rs` | Complete | — |
| HTTP/2 server lifecycle | — | — | Missing | HT-G4 |
| Multi-listener bind | — | — | Missing | HT-G5 |

### 5.5 T5.5 Server Lifecycle Closure Contract

The following contract rows close the scoped T5.5 parity requirements for
server startup, graceful shutdown, connection draining, and backpressure.

| Contract ID | Behavior | Success Path | Failure Path | Cancellation Path | Deterministic Assertion |
|-------------|----------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|-------------------------|
| `T55-LIFE-01` | Http1Listener bind and startup | `bind()` succeeds with valid address; listener enters Running phase | Invalid address or port-in-use returns `io::Error` | Cancel before accept loop starts drops listener cleanly | Bound address is deterministic for same input |
| `T55-LIFE-02` | Connection registration with capacity enforcement | Connection registered when under `max_connections` limit; `ConnectionGuard` returned | At capacity: `register()` returns None; connection rejected without counting | Registration during shutdown returns None (TOCTOU-safe double-check) | Connection ID is monotonically increasing |
| `T55-LIFE-03` | Graceful shutdown 4-phase state machine | `begin_drain()` transitions Running→Draining atomically (CAS); drain deadline set | Already draining: `begin_drain()` returns false | Each phase transition notifies waiters via `Notify` | Phase ordering is strict: Running < Draining < ForceClosing < Stopped |
| `T55-LIFE-04` | Connection drain with timeout | All connections close within drain timeout; `drain_with_stats()` returns `ShutdownStats` with drained count | Drain timeout exceeded: transitions to ForceClosing; remaining connections force-closed | Cancel during drain still tracks partial stats | Drained + force_closed = initial active count |
| `T55-LIFE-05` | Force close after drain timeout | `begin_force_close()` transitions Draining→ForceClosing; remaining connections terminated | Not in Draining state: returns false | Force close triggers immediate connection drop | State transition is atomic (single CAS) |
| `T55-LIFE-06` | Per-connection request limit enforcement | Connection serves up to `max_requests_per_connection` requests then closes gracefully | N/A (limit is soft close, not error) | Cancel mid-request does not increment served counter | Request count is per-connection monotonic |
| `T55-LIFE-07` | Idle timeout enforcement | Idle connection closed after `idle_timeout` between requests | N/A (timeout is soft close) | Cancel during idle wait drops connection cleanly | Timeout measured from last response completion |
| `T55-LIFE-08` | Keep-alive and Connection header semantics | HTTP/1.1 defaults to keep-alive; HTTP/1.0 defaults to close; `Connection: close` header honored | Client sends invalid Connection header: parsed but ignored | Cancel during keep-alive read closes connection | Close decision is deterministic for same config + headers |
| `T55-LIFE-09` | Transient accept error exponential backoff | Transient errors (WouldBlock, TimedOut, ConnectionRefused/Aborted/Reset, Interrupted) trigger backoff: 2ms base, 64ms cap | Fatal accept errors bubble up as `io::Error` | Shutdown signal interrupts backoff sleep | Backoff streak resets on successful accept |
| `T55-LIFE-10` | ConnectionGuard RAII lifecycle | Guard drop removes connection from HashMap and notifies drain waiters | N/A (Drop is infallible) | Panic during handler: guard still drops via unwind | Every `register()` has exactly one deregister via Drop |
| `T55-LIFE-11` | ShutdownStats accuracy | Stats record drained count, force-closed count, and total shutdown duration | N/A (stats collection is infallible) | Cancel before `collect_stats()` loses stats | duration = stopped_time - drain_start_time |
| `T55-LIFE-12` | with_graceful_shutdown race semantics | Future completes before shutdown: returns `GracefulOutcome::Completed(T)` | Shutdown signals before completion: returns `GracefulOutcome::ShutdownSignaled`; future dropped | Future cancellation propagates through Select combinator | Exactly one of Completed/ShutdownSignaled returned |

### 5.6 T5.5 Deterministic Scenario Pack

| Scenario ID | Scope | Expected Status | Required Log Fields |
|-------------|-------|-----------------|---------------------|
| `T55-LIFE-01` | Server bind to valid address | success | `scenario_id`, `addr`, `phase`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-02` | Server bind to in-use port | failure | `scenario_id`, `addr`, `error_kind`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-03` | Accept connection under capacity | success | `scenario_id`, `connection_id`, `active_count`, `max_connections`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-04` | Reject connection at capacity | failure | `scenario_id`, `active_count`, `max_connections`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-05` | Graceful drain completes within timeout | success | `scenario_id`, `drained`, `force_closed`, `duration_ms`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-06` | Drain timeout triggers force close | failure | `scenario_id`, `drained`, `force_closed`, `drain_timeout_ms`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-07` | begin_drain idempotency (already draining) | success | `scenario_id`, `already_draining`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-08` | Per-connection request limit reached | success | `scenario_id`, `requests_served`, `max_requests`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-09` | Idle timeout closes connection | success | `scenario_id`, `idle_duration_ms`, `idle_timeout_ms`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-10` | Keep-alive honored (HTTP/1.1 default) | success | `scenario_id`, `http_version`, `keep_alive`, `connection_header`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-11` | Connection close on HTTP/1.0 default | success | `scenario_id`, `http_version`, `keep_alive`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-12` | Transient accept error with backoff recovery | success | `scenario_id`, `error_count`, `backoff_ms`, `recovery`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-13` | ConnectionGuard drop during handler panic | success | `scenario_id`, `guard_dropped`, `connection_removed`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-14` | ShutdownStats accuracy after mixed drain | success | `scenario_id`, `drained`, `force_closed`, `total`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-15` | with_graceful_shutdown future completes first | success | `scenario_id`, `outcome`, `future_completed`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-16` | with_graceful_shutdown signal wins race | cancelled | `scenario_id`, `outcome`, `shutdown_signaled`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-17` | Shutdown rejects new connection registration | cancelled | `scenario_id`, `phase`, `register_result`, `outcome_class` |
| `T55-LIFE-18` | trigger_immediate hard shutdown | success | `scenario_id`, `phase_after`, `drain_skipped`, `outcome_class` |

| Close frame semantics | `net/websocket/close.rs` | Complete |
| Read/write splitting | `net/websocket/split.rs` | Complete |
| Client implementation | `net/websocket/client.rs` | Complete |

## 7. Migration Blocker Classification

### 7.1 Hard Blockers

| ID | Gap | Impact | Workaround |
|----|-----|--------|------------|
| WEB-G8 | Multipart form parsing | File upload endpoints broken | Manual parsing of multipart boundary |
| MW-G7 | Static file serving | Cannot serve frontend assets | External reverse proxy |
| GRPC-G3 | Protobuf codegen | Must write service stubs manually | Manual trait implementation |
| GRPC-G9 | Deadline propagation | gRPC timeout contracts not enforced | Manual timeout insertion |

### 7.2 Soft Blockers

| ID | Gap | Impact |
|----|-----|--------|
| WEB-G1 | Router merge | Cannot compose independently-developed routers |
| ~~WEB-G2~~ | ~~Fallback handler~~ | Closed — `.fallback()` implemented in router |
| WEB-G12 | SSE support | Server-Sent Events endpoints broken |
| ~~MW-G2~~ | ~~CORS middleware~~ | Closed — `CorsMiddleware` + `CorsPolicy` implemented |
| MW-G1 | Decompression layer | Response compression complete; request decompression still missing |
| MW-G3 | Trace layer | No structured request tracing out of box |
| ~~GRPC-G1~~ | ~~Reflection service~~ | Closed — `ReflectionService` implemented in `grpc/reflection.rs` |
| ~~GRPC-G2~~ | ~~Message compression~~ | Closed — gzip frame compress/decompress in `grpc/codec.rs` |
| GRPC-G5 | Client-side streaming | Client streaming and bidi patterns limited |
| GRPC-G6 | Load balancing | Single-server only |

### 7.3 Non-Blocking

| ID | Gap | Notes |
|----|-----|-------|
| WEB-G3 | Wildcard routes | Niche; most apps use named params |
| WEB-G7 | MatchedPath/OriginalUri/Host | Diagnostic extractors |
| ~~MW-G6~~ | ~~NormalizePath~~ | Closed — `NormalizePathMiddleware` implemented |
| ~~MW-G9~~ | ~~CatchPanic~~ | Closed — `CatchPanicMiddleware` implemented |
| HT-G2 | HTTP/2 server push | Deprecated by browsers |
| HT-G3 | HTTP/2 priority | Largely ignored by implementations |

---

## 8. Ownership Boundaries by Module

| Domain | Primary Surface | Boundary Contract | Owning Bead(s) |
|--------|-----------------|-------------------|----------------|
| Router + extractors + responses | `src/web/router.rs`, `src/web/extract.rs`, `src/web/response.rs` | `web/` owns request matching, extraction, and response typing. `service/` layers must not duplicate route semantics. | `asupersync-2oh2u.5.1`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.2`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.3` |
| Middleware composition | `src/service/*`, `src/web/middleware.rs` | `service/` owns generic layer traits/builders; `web/middleware.rs` owns HTTP-specialized adapters only. Middleware stack contracts closed by T5.4. | `asupersync-2oh2u.5.1`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.3`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.4` |
| gRPC server/client/protocol | `src/grpc/*` | `grpc/` owns method dispatch, metadata, and RPC semantics; transport lives below in `http/` + `net/`. | `asupersync-2oh2u.5.1`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.6`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.7` |
| HTTP transport + pooling + lifecycle | `src/http/*`, `src/net/tcp/*`, `src/server/*` | `http/` owns wire protocol correctness/backpressure; `server/` owns shutdown/drain lifecycle; `web/` and `grpc/` consume transport contracts, never bypass them. Server lifecycle contracts closed by T5.5. | `asupersync-2oh2u.5.1`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.5`, `asupersync-2oh2u.5.8` |
| Cross-layer observability | `src/observability/*`, `src/trace/*` | All layers emit structured events with trace correlation; no ambient stdout/stderr in runtime paths. | `asupersync-2oh2u.5.1`, `asupersync-2oh2u.10.12` |

---

## 9. Asupersync-Specific Extensions (No Tokio Equivalent)

| Feature | Location | Purpose |
|---------|----------|---------|
| Request-scoped regions | `web/request_region.rs` | Structured cleanup on disconnect |
| `AsupersyncService` trait | `service/service.rs` | Cancel-aware service with `Cx` parameter |
| `CircuitBreakerMiddleware` | `web/middleware.rs` | Built-in circuit breaker |
| `BulkheadMiddleware` | `web/middleware.rs` | Built-in concurrency isolation |
| Debug server | `web/debug.rs` | Introspection endpoints |
| Next.js bootstrap | `web/nextjs_bootstrap.rs` | SSR integration |
| Browser transport caps | `io/cap.rs` | WASM fetch/transport capabilities |
| gRPC `HealthReporter` | `grpc/health.rs` | Dynamic health status updates |
| gRPC-Web protocol | `grpc/web.rs` | HTTP/1.1 transport for gRPC (binary + base64 text modes) |
| gRPC reflection | `grpc/reflection.rs` | Runtime service discovery and introspection |
| gRPC frame compression | `grpc/codec.rs` | Gzip compression/decompression with bomb protection |

---

### 9.1 T5.7 gRPC Production Features Closure Contract

The following production features are required for tonic-equivalent gRPC deployment:

| Feature | Implementation | Module | Tests | Status |
|---------|---------------|--------|-------|--------|
| Health checking protocol | `HealthService`, `HealthServiceBuilder`, `HealthReporter`, `ServingStatus` | `grpc/health.rs` | `health::tests` (in-module) | Complete |
| Server reflection | `ReflectionService`, `ReflectedService`, `ReflectedMethod` | `grpc/reflection.rs` | `tests/grpc_enhancement_integration.rs::reflection` | Complete |
| Gzip frame compression | `gzip_frame_compress`, `gzip_frame_decompress` (feature `compression`) | `grpc/codec.rs` | `tests/grpc_enhancement_integration.rs::compression` | Complete |
| Server compression config | `ServerConfig::send_compression`, `accept_compression` | `grpc/server.rs` | `tests/grpc_enhancement_integration.rs::cross_feature` | Complete |
| Client compression negotiation | `CompressionEncoding::from_header_value`, `frame_compressor`, `frame_decompressor` | `grpc/client.rs` | `tests/grpc_enhancement_integration.rs::compression` | Complete |
| Interceptor stack | `InterceptorLayer`, `BearerAuthInterceptor`, `LoggingInterceptor`, `RateLimitInterceptor`, `TimeoutInterceptor`, `TracingInterceptor`, `MetadataPropagator` | `grpc/interceptor.rs` | In-module tests | Complete |
| gRPC-Web protocol | `WebFrameCodec`, `TrailerFrame`, `ContentType`, binary + base64 text modes | `grpc/web.rs` | `tests/grpc_enhancement_integration.rs::grpc_web` + in-module (21 tests) | Complete |
| Deadline propagation | — | — | — | Missing (GRPC-G9) |

**Evidence commands**:
```bash
rch exec -- cargo test --test grpc_enhancement_integration -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --lib grpc::codec::tests -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --lib grpc::web::tests -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --lib grpc::health::tests -- --nocapture
```

**Remaining T5.7 gaps**: Only GRPC-G9 (deadline propagation) is missing. All other tonic production features have complete implementations with deterministic tests.

---

**Total**: 34 open gaps (5 High, 12 Medium, 17 Low) — 11 closed (WEB-G2, WEB-G10, MW-G2, MW-G5, MW-G6, MW-G8, MW-G9, GRPC-G1, GRPC-G2, GRPC-G11, HT-G1)

---

## 11. Recommended Execution Order

### Phase A: Hard Blockers (Weeks 1-3)

1. **MW-G7**: ServeDir/ServeFile — blocks frontend asset serving
3. **WEB-G8**: Multipart form parsing — blocks file upload
4. **WEB-G12**: SSE support — blocks real-time event endpoints
5. **GRPC-G3**: Protobuf codegen — blocks ergonomic gRPC service definition
6. **GRPC-G9**: Deadline propagation — blocks production gRPC correctness
7. **GRPC-G5**: Client streaming completion — blocks streaming RPC patterns

### Phase B: Ergonomics (Weeks 3-6)

8. **WEB-G1/G15**: Router merge + filter-combinator parity
9. **WEB-G5/G16**: TypedHeader + typed-path/boxed ergonomics
10. **MW-G1**: Decompression layer (compression complete)
11. **MW-G3**: TraceLayer for structured request tracing
12. **~~GRPC-G2~~/G7/G8**: ~~Compression (closed)~~, pooling, retry for clients

### Phase C: Production Polish (Weeks 6-8)

14. Remaining medium gaps

### Phase D: Nice-to-Have (Defer)

15. Low-severity gaps as capacity allows

---

## 12. Evidence and Validation Commands

The following commands are the canonical reproducibility bundle for this parity map:

```bash
br show asupersync-2oh2u.5.1 --json
br show asupersync-2oh2u.5.2 --json
br show asupersync-2oh2u.5.3 --json
br show asupersync-2oh2u.5.4 --json
br show asupersync-2oh2u.5.5 --json
rch exec -- cargo test --test tokio_web_grpc_parity_map -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --lib web::extract::tests -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --lib web::middleware::tests -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --test web_router_composition -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --test web_router_match_order -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --test grpc_enhancement_integration -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --lib grpc::web::tests -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --lib grpc::codec::tests -- --nocapture
rg -n "WEB-G|MW-G|GRPC-G|HT-G|WS-G" docs/tokio_web_grpc_parity_map.md
```

Expected evidence artifacts:
- `docs/tokio_web_grpc_parity_map.md` (canonical parity contract + blocker classification + ownership boundaries)
- `tests/tokio_web_grpc_parity_map.rs` (contract checks)
- `src/web/router.rs` (router composition + match-order implementation surface)
- `src/web/extract.rs` (parameter and wildcard extraction semantics)
- `src/web/middleware.rs` (fallback + layered dispatch interplay)
- CI test logs for `tokio_web_grpc_parity_map` test target

| 2026-03-03 | WhiteDesert | Added T5.2 router composition + route-matching closure contract, deterministic scenario pack, and validation/evidence expansion (v1.2) |
| 2026-03-03 | SapphireHill | Added T5.3 extractor/body handling closure contract (10 contract rows, 20 scenario pack entries), hardened Form body size limit, closed WEB-G2 + WEB-G10 as implemented (v1.3) |
| 2026-03-03 | SapphireHill | Added T5.4 middleware stack closure contract (17 contract rows, 24 scenario pack entries), closed MW-G2 + MW-G5 + MW-G6 + MW-G8 + MW-G9 as implemented, updated blocker classification (v1.4) |
| 2026-03-03 | SapphireHill | Added T5.5 server lifecycle closure contract (12 contract rows, 18 scenario pack entries), added server lifecycle parity table, identified HT-G4 + HT-G5 new gaps (v1.5) |
| 2026-03-04 | SapphireHill | Added T5.7 gRPC production features closure contract, closed GRPC-G1 (reflection), GRPC-G2 (compression), GRPC-G11 (binary metadata), HT-G1 (100-continue), added gRPC-Web capability surface, updated gap count 38→34 (v1.6) |
